programme.title: Cold Blood
programme.desc: The police investigation leading to the capture of the Gainesville Ripper, a serial killer who murdered five students in a small college town in Florida. The trail had stalled when officers initially failed to establish a forensic link, but when Daniel Rolling was arrested for an apparently unrelated burglary incident, a match was made between his belongings and the murder scenes. Rolling later confessed to three additional murders in Louisiana, and an attempt on his father's life(n)

programme.desc: Lofty and Dom prepare to renew their wedding vows with some special guests - but Lofty has begun to question the validity of their marriage, and is forced to consider what he really wants out of life. Sacha's daughter gives some tough advice, and Chloe tries to decide what she wants to do about her pregnancy, and realises she must tell Ange everything if she is to find peace. Casualty stars Amanda Henderson and Jamie Davis make a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Julia must decide whether she is able to go through with her wedding day in light of what she has learned about Benjamin. She tries to confide in Della, only to discover that her daughter has come to empathise with her loyalty to Benjamin and been inspired to make a positive change in her own life. As she realises that cancelling the wedding risks causing her daughter pain, she turns to Ted, who tries to persuade her to give their relationship another chance. However, she rejects his fantasy of a happy past and forces him to come to terms with what he did to her(n)

programme.title: Inside the Cage: The Rise of Female Fighters
programme.desc: Annie Price meets the women fighting to the top in Mixed Martial Arts, one of the UK's fastest growing sports, finding out whether the risks of the sport are worth the reward. Annie meets Molly McCann, the first English woman to win in the Ultimate Fighting Championship, and Cory McKenna, a 19-year-old former student who has left her studies behind to become a full-time fighter(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is both payday and Halloween party time at the salon. Tom gets a tepid welcome from the girls as Sammyjo tries to find out where she stands(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Saffron tells Nan about her budding romance. There is another shock for Sammyjo, and she seeks outside help to figure out what to do(n)

programme.title: A Most Violent Year (2014)
programme.desc: New York City during the winter of 1981 is a dangerous place to be, but one immigrant is determined to expand his business and support his family. Unfortunately, the unrelenting rise in violent crime threatens to ruin his livelihood and destroy everything he has worked tirelessly for. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain and David Oyelowo(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark arranges a singing flash-mob with a group of dog walkers to prove that shared experiences like singing can make people happy and feel more bonded(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a semi-detached in Dover, Kent, and a parcel of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts explores the potential of a terrace in Tonypandy, south Wales(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dental director Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues who has two troublesome wisdom teeth that need to be removed in surgery(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, including a massage candle, a reusable hot drinks lid, and a board game. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Anita Manning and experts Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw get into the panto spirit at Newark Antiques Fair for a festive edition of the show, helping the teams spend £300 on three items to take to auction in Derbyshire. Away from the contest, Anita joins a band for a seasonal sing-a-long and looks into the history of Christmas cards(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi is arrested when a stash of drugs are found in his car. Meanwhile, Daniel has to step in when he encounters a man who is gambling with his own life(n)

programme.title: The Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Part two of two. With police detectives seemingly no closer to closing the murder cases that have shaken Ballarat, their suspicion is aroused by some articles about unsolved cases in Ballarat - could these murders be linked to something long ago? Meanwhile, Jean's compassion is the only thing standing in the way of Matthew convicting the man who fell through the ceiling of the abandoned house. Australian mystery drama, starring Nadine Garner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman finds herself in the east Devon countryside with a couple who have £750,000 to find a more pastoral life and business. While in the county, Nicki goes deep into the woods to meet an artist who transforms fallen timber into beautiful bespoke winged sculptures(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry compete to design a family garden for a Cardiff couple, coming up with ideas that blend spaces for entertaining with room for two growing children(n)

programme.desc: The experts bring a 1930s portrait of Japanese martial arts master Yukio Tani back to life, rebuild a Victorian nursery chair and reconstruct an ornate porcelain clock(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: Tom Allen meets this year's winner and runner-up, as well as Lord Sugar, to look back over the past 12 weeks of the business-based reality show. Featuring contributions from former contestants and special guests(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Ashleigh gets approved to buy a brand new house, and Sammyjo takes a big step in getting over Tom(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North

programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life as the team takes on a broken reel-to-reel tape recording machine, a costume that once belonged to a 1950s strong woman and a wind-up car. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.desc: Crews tend to a 95-year-old Navy veteran as he is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He's diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. Meanwhile. a man has his holiday interrupted with kidney problems. Narrated by Kevin Whately(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Cocker Spaniel Tilly has been in labour for 12 hours and is struggling to give birth. It's a potentially life-threatening situation, so vet Maurice King decides to perform an urgent C-section to deliver a valuable litter of pups. A young jackdaw is brought into the clinic unable to fly. The vets rehome him with a local woman who has Asperger's syndrome, who has a particular affinity with animals. At the Downpatrick clinic Dexter - a therapy dog at the local hospital - is brought in to see vet Rachael Frew. Dexter has had cancerous lumps on his skin before and more have recently appeared(n)

Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, with Celia Johnson, Trevor Howard and Cyril Raymond(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman explore the fascinating factory processes and surprising history behind the nation's favourite festive treats. Gregg follows 24 hours of production at a factory in Oldham, Greater Manchester, which will be making two million Christmas cakes for Marks and Spencer, while Cherry visits Britain's largest marzipan manufacturer as well as a sprout farm. Ruth investigates how the UK's early industrial heritage inspired Charles Dickens to write A Christmas Carol(n)

King Kong, 7. Carol Cleveland, 6. Extrasensory perception(n)

programme.title: Responsible Child
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following the story of Ray, a boy on the cusp of adolescence who is arrested along with his adult brother Nathan for the murder of his mum's abusive partner. With the age of criminal responsibility set at 10, Ray stands trial for murder in an adult courtroom. The film follows both the events leading up to the crime, and the boy's experience of the trial. Starring Billy Barratt, Michelle Fairley, Tom Burke and James Tarpey(n)

programme.desc: A widow trying to escape scandalous rumours resolves to find suitable husbands for herself and her daughter. Period comedy, starring Kate Beckinsale and Chloe Sevigny(n)

They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser goes into communities around Wales and creates choirs that will benefit people who feel isolated such as stay-at-home parents, retirees, those who are cut off by transport links, and people with mobility issues, teaching them to sing so they can put on a performance(n)

programme.desc: A specialist surgeon travels to Shetland to carry out a complex operation, while a Fair Isle man hopes to get the all-clear after surgery to find out if cancer has been kept at bay(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along the eastern coast of Australia from the coal mines of Newcastle, New South Wales, to Queensland's modern state capital, Brisbane. In the world's largest coal-exporting port, Michael marvels at the skill of the loader as he transfers thousands of tons of coal from rail to ship. He visits the Koala Hospital at Port Macquarie, where he finds populations of these marsupials are in rapid decline. Michael also visits a factory where the quintessential Australian bush hat is made and in Woolgoolga, he joins a Sikh community to hear how they have integrated into Australian society(n)

The pair travel in a 1970s Morris Marina Coupe, get competitive in a football match, play a game of air hockey in the arcade, and compete in Segway racing(n)

programme.title: Those Magnificent Men in Their Flying Machines (1965)
programme.desc: A publisher offers to sponsor the first London-to-Paris air race in a bid to boost the sales of his newspaper. Among the motley assortment of characters who sign up for the challenge is a scheming nobleman bent on sabotaging the efforts of the other entrants. Period comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, Sarah Miles, James Fox, Robert Morley, Benny Hill, Tony Hancock, Eric Sykes and Gert Frobe(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram value some Spiritualist photographs and a Hornby train set at Culzean Castle, while Paul Martin follows in the footsteps of Robert Burns(n)

programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first episode of finals week, in which the four remaining contestants must cook for an exclusive gathering of some of the nation's greatest chefs. Each of the contenders takes responsibility for one course of the four-course meal, under the watchful eye of judges Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti(n)

programme.title: The Brexit Storm Continues: Laura Kuenssberg's Inside Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the tumultuous events of Boris Johnson's Conservative government. The programme follows his appointment as leader of the Conservative party, attempts to secure a Brexit deal with the European Union, the attempted proroguing of parliament, an act subsequently declared unlawful, and the eventual calling of a general election. Featuring contributions from MPs on all sides, who reveal the pressure they have come under from an increasingly divided and frustrated electorate(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: As the dust settles after the first winter General Election in decades, Fiona Bruce is joined in London by guests from across the political spectrum to ascertain what changes the outcome is likely to bring. The main focus is Brexit, and what changes are most likely for the United Kingdom in terms of its future relationship with the European Union. However, the campaign also turned up a lot of other issues regarding the NHS, public spending and defence, and all these matters are likely to be debated in this special edition(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the second episode, cameras follow new parents Mo and Syler as they get to grips with round-the-clock feeding and sleep deprivation, and Paul and Craig's surrogate Mel gives birth to their twins. programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, PC Mick Johnson is stabbed in the arm as he tries to help a shopkeeper trapped inside a convenience store with a knife-wielding man, while a police officer in North Wales is knocked down by a driver trying to resist arrest. In Bradford, firefighter Phil Warden has his final bout of surgery following 10 years of treatment after a missile thrown through a fire engine window broke his nose in 11 places(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel to the small town of Deri near Caerphilly to celebrate the success of its rugby club, which has gone from the brink of extinction just four years ago to being crowned league champions. To mark the achievements of the players and the volunteers who run the club, Sam and Shauna cook up a huge venison feast with their help(n)

programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by Chris Bavin as they share a selection of festive recipes, including stuffed roast turkey and French toast with smoked bacon(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing head judge Len Goodman takes actress Lisa Riley - best known to Emmerdale fans as Mandy Dingle on a journey to Bronte Country - Haworth in West Yorkshire - to re-create a family trip there in 1992. The pair travel in a Citroen CX, just like the one Lisa's mum drove all those years ago, and after the obligatory Barbra Streisand singalong, Lisa explains the importance of the Bronte sisters on her childhood. Along the way, Len and Lisa take a trip on a steam trip, visit the Bronte memorial chapel and chat about the actress's career(n)

programme.title: Swallows and Amazons (1974)
programme.desc: Four youngsters go boating while on holiday in the Lake District where they encounter two more children with a boat of their own, and a rivalry develops over a series of escapades. Family adventure, based on Arthur Ransome's classic novel, starring Virginia McKenna, Ronald Fraser, Sophie Neville and Simon West(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford joins Si and Dave as they demonstrate festive recipes using leftovers, including Christmas pudding trifle, bubble and squeak, tartiflette, gougere, and turkey pie(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Monterrey or Al-Sadd v Liverpool (Kick-off 5.30pm). All the action from the semi-final at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Liverpool will be looking to go one better than in their previous appearance in this competition, when they finished as runners-up to Sao Paolo in 2005, and they will expect to prevail this evening regardless of which opponent they face and progress to Saturday's final(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Porthcawl crew have just seconds to spare when two teenagers on holiday get caught on a rocky outcrop by a large swell and a rising spring tide, while in East Yorkshire, the Flamborough crew have to pluck a man from danger after he jumps off a 100 foot cliff for a dare. In Devon, the seas are often crowded with sailing craft of all shapes and sizes and when two collide, the Torbay lifeboat crew are called out to provide emergency medical assistance for a potentially life-changing injury(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the penultimate challenge, the four remaining chefs are challenged to create a single exceptional dish, inspired by a place that is special to them, as they fight to secure their place in the final three. After one contestant is sent home, the three remaining chefs travel to Lisbon to experience cooking alongside the acclaimed chef José Avillez. First, they take a trip to an historical food market, where they find ingredients to create their own dish for Chef José to taste, before joining José's brigade of chefs during a packed lunchtime service(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the families emerge from the first few hectic weeks with their new babies and realise that they need to re-evaluate their priorities. Paul and Craig strive to be truly equal parents, and Eilidh and Richard wonder whether they will ever get to take their baby home. Meanwhile, Mel returns to see the twins(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: The Blair Witch Project's Heather Donahue remembers the final year of the millennium, which saw her ultra-low-budget horror film terrify audiences the world over. Britney Spears shot to stardom with Hit Me Baby One More Time, director Baz Luhrmann offered the year's most curious entry into the pop charts, Sunscreen, and Channel 4 drama Queer as Folk arrived amid a storm of controversy. Kelly Brook began her short-lived stint on The Big Breakfast, Fatboy Slim's video for Praise You won critical acclaim and energy drink Red Bull outsold Coca-Cola as the millennium celebrations loomed(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.desc: Christmas specials starring Diane Morgan as the incredibly stupid documentary-maker Philomena Cunk(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.desc: Steve Wright introduces a selection of archive studio performances and videos featuring the Spice Girls, Michael Jackson, Pulp, Shania Twain, Bryan Adams, the Prodigy, Madonna, Blur, Take That and others(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams made up of distinguished graduates of Sheffield and Manchester compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. Among those facing Jeremy Paxman's questions are politician David Blunkett and political commentator Paul Mason for Sheffield, with journalist David Aaronovitch and Watchdog presenter Matt Allwright representing Manchester(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Ben Garrod explores how skin has evolved to enable animals to move in a range of habitats, revealing how adaptations allow birds to fly, sharks to speed through water and sugar gliders to take to the air.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Juliet Stevenson narrates a documentary filmed over the course of a year that tells the stories of the animals and people inhabiting Icleand. An Arctic fox family ekes out a clifftop living, an eider farmer has his hands full with the arrival of a host of ducklings and Viking horsemen prepare to saddle up for the autumn round-up. However, nature's clock is ticking and the constant volcanic threat eventually boils over with one of the island's biggest eruptions in more than 200 years(n)

programme.title: Toy Empire: The British Force Behind Star Wars Toys
programme.desc: David Whiteley reveals how an unassuming band of designers and factory workers in Coalville, Leicestershire produced the Star Wars action figures that were to define a generation. David meets the Palitoy employees workers who succeeded in getting the toys on the shelves against all the odds, and the people whose collections of them are now worth eye-watering sums(n)

programme.title: The Galaxy Britain Built: The British Force Behind Star Wars
programme.desc: David Whiteley celebrates the unsung British heroes behind the first film in the sci-fi franchise, including the contribution of the London Symphony Orchestra of the London Symphony Orchestra. He also meets Ann Skinner, who was in charge of continuity, who reveals how she helped Alec Guinness with one of the most famous speeches in Star Wars(n)

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks
programme.desc: Hollywood composers including Hans Zimmer, James Cameron, Danny Elfman, John Williams, Quincy Jones and Trent Reznor combine to give a privileged look inside the musical challenges and creative secrecy of the film score(n)

programme.title: Cleopatra and Me: In Search of a Lost Queen
programme.desc: Shakespeare scholar Islam Issa grew up in Birmingham with his Egyptian-born parents. As a child he was taught Cleopatra was a great politician, who turned her capitol Alexandria into a global seat of learning and made Egypt one of the most powerful countries on the planet. But as a teenager, he realised she had a very different reputation in Britain - either a tragic Shakespearean heroine or a seductress, portrayed by screen sirens like Elizabeth Taylor. In this programme, he sets out to discover the story behind these two Cleopatras(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas is determined to expand the legal and illegal sides of the Shelby family business by taking on various interests in the south of England, but an enemy from his past looks set to stand in his way. Cillian Murphy stars(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Enigmatic mobster Alfie Solomons crosses paths with Tommy while the latter is in London searching for Polly's long-lost children. Solomons is a dangerous man, and Tommy will be lucky to get away unscathed. Meanwhile, ghosts from Arthur's military past continue to haunt his troubled mind(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy comes up with a plan to take control of the southern racecourses, and also sees an opportunity to move up in the world, and has his head turned by the aristocratic May Carleton. However, London gangster Darby Sabini and Major Campbell are plotting the downfall of the Shelbys(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as teams made up of distinguished graduates of Edinburgh University and the London School of Economics compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. Edinburgh is represented by the likes of MP Joanna Cherry and comedian Mitch Benn, while the LSE team includes critic Ekow Eshun and writer Maya Jaggi(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Ben Garrod explores how skin acts as a barrier between animals and the outside world, shielding them from extremes of temperature, the harmful effects of UV and a host of organisms(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how animals all over the world rise to the challenge of surviving the winter. While some creatures face an up-hill struggle to survive, a colder climate plays to the advantage of others, who quickly set about making use of inventive means to seize the huge opportunities the season brings. Caribou become 'ice road' travellers as the ground under-foot becomes slippery, and stoats build their own fur beds, snow monkeys set about finding warm baths, and even Emperor penguins - who are build to face extreme low temperatures - find their own way to endure the world's most savage winter conditions. Narrated by Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Three-part documentary about the execution of Charles I. The first episode follows the events of Christmas 1648, when the king was visited at Windsor by the Earl of Denbigh, seeking to broker terms between the imprisoned monarch and an increasingly hardline parliament. When his visit was rejected, new legislation is drawn up to try the king for treason(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating handcrafted objects made under the royal warrant, beginning by following the creation of a Wedgwood panther vase by artisan potters. The programme also takes a look at how this quintessentially British company is reinventing itself for the 21st century under new, foreign ownership(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: A look at the work of shoemakers John Lobb, which has produced hand-crafted footwear for gentlemen since the 19th century and boasts Royal Warrants from Prince Philip and Prince Charles. The company uses methods that have barely changed since it was founded, from pencilled outlines on brown paper, to the cutting and stitching of leather and the final polishing. Zoe Wanamaker narrates(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Dr Thomas Asbridge looks at how TV's telling of the Crusades has changed over the past 60 years. Using footage from Crusade documentaries, he reveals how modern political and social change have influenced its interpretation, and asks whether this 1,000-year-old story really does cast a shadow over the modern world(n)

programme.title: Looking for Rembrandt
programme.desc: Desperate for cash, the artist takes on commissions that his pupils have rejected, and looks for creative ways to divert money into his pocket as all of his possessions are taken by creditors. Rembrandt's work takes on a new style, embracing humanity rather than hubris - but this is not to everyone's taste(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Arthur leads the Peaky Blinders in their plot to take over London's Eden Club, and an angry Sabini manages to convince Alfie to put their feud to one side and help him destroy the gang. Tommy hires society darling May Carleton to train his race horse and they quickly form a close relationship, and he later discovers the truth about the mission he is being forced to undertake for Major Campbell. Is he on the verge of losing everything as he becomes embroiled in Campbell's political game? Back in Birmingham, Polly's son Michael soon discovers the dark side of the family business(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy's power base in London is obliterated and Arthur and Michael are imprisoned as Major Campbell makes his influence felt. Distraught at the prospect of losing her son to a lengthy spell in prison, Polly compromises herself to secure Michael's freedom. However, her actions risk damaging already fragile relationships. While attempting to regain the upper hand, Tommy is paid a visit by someone from his past. Old wounds are reopened and he finds himself having to make an impossible decision. Starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: It is the day of the Epsom Derby and Tommy is there to carry out the mission given to him by Major Campbell, but he plans to turn the situation to his advantage by bringing his gang to the racecourse and striking a blow against Sabini. However, more than one of his adversaries have forged secret schemes, and as he readies his troops, Tommy receives shocking news that will change everything. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the first semi-final as teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive edition(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod examines how animals use their skin to send out messages to their own species, and warning signals to others(n)

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold examine the festive season as it would have been celebrated in 1500 under the reign of Henry VII, concentrating on Christmas Day, New Year's Day and Twelfth Night. They make Tudor decorations, engage in festive revels and prepare feasting delights such as boar's head and shred pies - which were forerunners of mince pies and were made with meat. They also investigate the Tudor origins of Christmas carols, the singing of which was known as wassailing, and learn about the Lord of Misrule - traditionally a commoner placed in authority over his social betters for the festive period and in charge of directing the Christmas revelry(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Three-part documentary focusing on the monarch's final days. In the second episode, the country prepares for the unprecedented trial of the King. Although the consensus is that the King is a tyrant, only half of the commissioners appointed actually attend the meetings at Westminster Palace, in fear of being indicted as traitors. On the 20th of January 1649, the three day trial begins, and the King and the Lord President John Bradshaw become embroiled in a battle of wills(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The adventures of colourful characters in a magical, musical world(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser goes into communities around Wales and creates choirs for people who feel isolated, beginning by setting one up in a rural area. Mark meets Dossie, a villager who now lives alone after her husband passed away, and Ella, who has recently moved back with her parents after university and is struggling to make new friends. Can he pull a group together not only to put on a performance of their lifetimes, but to help them make lasting connections with a real impact?(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a detached house in Gillingham, Kent, she first saw in 2017. Plus, a semi-detached in Walsall, West Midlands, and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A specialist surgeon travels to Shetland to carry out a complex operation, while a Fair Isle man hopes to get the all-clear after surgery to find out if cancer has been kept at bay(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: Vets Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes help to artificially inseminate a precious Arabian mare. Philippa also has to create tiny plastic shoes to help one of her smallest patients - a Shetland pony foal - cure its bandy legs, and the clinic team works hard in the operating theatre to treat a deadly infection threatening the life of a much-loved family pet(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross visits York Theatre Royal to learn about the history of pantomime(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma struggles to make sense of what has happened, while Ruhma has surprise news for a pregnant woman in police custody(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Following reports of Flambeau's death, the clerical sleuth races to protect a crown that contains a nail from the crucifixion of Christ. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to South Wales with a couple who are looking for a country home after three decades of military housing, and have a budget of £430,000 to spend. Away from the property search, Jules dons his own army fatigues to discover how the beautiful Welsh wilderness prepares soldiers for battle(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry face a challenge in Liphook, Hampshire, where a couple have a big garden and a daughter with her own ideas on what makes the perfect outdoor space(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Lucia Scalisi tries to mend a serious tear in a portrait, William Kirk and Steven Fletcher join forces to fix a walking stick and Brenton West tackles a seaside telescope(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is sure Sharon and Jack are having an affair and sets out for revenge, despite Ben's efforts to convince him otherwise. Martin is on a high following another job with Tubbs, only for Sonia to bring him down when she quizzes him about his sudden wealth. Mick is frustrated when Ollie does not land a main role in the school nativity so he plans his own version - but Linda, Tina and Shirley make him realise it will not be quite so easy. Jay entrusts Stuart to manage the undertaker's(n)

programme.title: A Berry Royal Christmas
programme.desc: This one-off programme offers a unique look into charities that the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge support, as they invite Mary Berry to accompany them on four special visits. Mary also prepares a feast for a Christmas party hosted by the royal couple for the volunteers and gets help preparing the dishes from special guests including former Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain - and the Duke and Duchess themselves. Throughout the programme Mary also cooks some of her favourite Christmas recipes for viewers to try out at home(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen looks through the archives to see how Wales has celebrated the festive season over the past 50 years. Featuring a grandmother who had her own festive grotto, a Christmas baby from 1971, a seasonal food hamper being sent to the Skerrie Islanders and a bus driver who keeps his passengers in the mood by wearing a tree on his head(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past five years, a radical new church has been promising hundreds of young people an escape from gangs and crime and a pathway to salvation through prosperity and success. SPAC Nation, the Salvation Proclaimers Anointed Church, holds services in hotels, conference halls and football grounds and leader Tobi Adegboyega has been courted by politicians and the media for his work in tackling gang violence. But Panorama has discovered how the church's outward show of concern for young people may hide a less than charitable appetite for money. Reporter Greg McKenzie investigates its fundraising activities and speaks to young worshippers who say they've been forced into debt whilst church leaders enjoy lavish lifestyles(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The baby is due soon and Siobhan is upset with the nonstop arguing of both families, so Orla tries to come up with a solution. She suggests that everyone go away for a weekend break together to neighbouring Co Kerry and try to learn to get along for Siobhan and the baby's sake, but no-one seems convinced this is a good idea(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The festive season arrives once again in the Brown household, and Father Damian has organised a Christmas decoration contest to support the local football team. Agnes is keen to enter, especially as it might mean getting one over her old nemesis, Hillary Nicholson, but is soon distracted by her new 'wi-fi assistant' and quite what to do with it(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark explores the Welsh valleys in search of members for his choir, including a woman who was born in the Bahamas, and misses the strong sense of community(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a mid-terrace in Birmingham, and a detached property in Hounslow, London, and cameras return to a plot in Bury, Greater Manchester(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A patient arrives in A&E with mysterious abdominal pains, and Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues, who has two wisdom teeth that need to be removed(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time he's on a quest to discover the truth about people's relationship with animals. It's a story of fat cats, excitable guinea-pigs and musical dogs, full of fascinating creatures from the nation's past(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw presents from the Westpoint Arena in Exeter as two people take on their former teacher and her husband, with help from experts Gary Pe and Charlie Ross(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel supports Zara as she ends things with Adam, while Al and Jimmi have a threatening visitor at The Icon. Ayesha deals with the fall-out from a batch of dodgy pills on campus(n)

programme.title: The Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: First in a two-part sequel to The Doctor Blake Mysteries. The year is 1963, and Dr Lucien Blake is missing, presumed dead, leaving Jean behind and bereaved in a changing Ballarat. But when Chief Superintendent Matthew Lawson is confronted with two grisly murders, Jean is determined to prove that she is more than equal to her husband when it comes to solving such a mystery. After all, no one knows this town better than her. Crime drama, starring Nadine Garner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is house-hunting in Somerset with a couple who have a £675,000 budget for a rural home that must have annexe potential for their parents. During his time in the county, Jules visits the Royal Navy's Fleet Air Arm Museum, where he gets up close and personal with an ambitious project to rebuild a Second World War aircraft using reclaimed parts(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock helps to create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing an adult relaxation area for parents Michelle and Alistair(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A music box housed in a replica Swiss chalet, a miniature globe from the 1800s and a broken garnet bracelet are pieced back together by the experts. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil goes to extreme lengths to show he is not to be messed with, leaving Jack in terrible danger. Mick and Mitch plan their alternative nativity - and get help from an unexpected source. The Panesar brothers burst into the Slaters with some shocking news - they are buying their house - and Karen suggests Janet should enter a beauty contest, despite Honey's concerns(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lofty and Dom prepare to renew their wedding vows with some special guests - but Lofty has begun to question the validity of their marriage, and is forced to consider what he really wants out of life. Sacha's daughter gives some tough advice, and Chloe tries to decide what she wants to do about her pregnancy, and realises she must tell Ange everything if she is to find peace. Casualty stars Amanda Henderson and Jamie Davis make a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Julia must decide whether she is able to go through with her wedding day in light of what she has learned about Benjamin. She tries to confide in Della, only to discover that her daughter has come to empathise with her loyalty to Benjamin and been inspired to make a positive change in her own life. As she realises that cancelling the wedding risks causing her daughter pain, she turns to Ted, who tries to persuade her to give their relationship another chance. However, she rejects his fantasy of a happy past and forces him to come to terms with what he did to her(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Inside the Cage: The Rise of Female Fighters
programme.desc: Annie Price meets the women fighting to the top in Mixed Martial Arts, one of the UK's fastest growing sports, finding out whether the risks of the sport are worth the reward. Annie meets Molly McCann, the first English woman to win in the Ultimate Fighting Championship, and Cory McKenna, a 19-year-old former student who has left her studies behind to become a full-time fighter(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is both payday and Halloween party time at the salon. Tom gets a tepid welcome from the girls as Sammyjo tries to find out where she stands(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Saffron tells Nan about her budding romance. There is another shock for Sammyjo, and she seeks outside help to figure out what to do(n)

programme.title: A Most Violent Year (2014)
programme.desc: New York City during the winter of 1981 is a dangerous place to be, but one immigrant is determined to expand his business and support his family. Unfortunately, the unrelenting rise in violent crime threatens to ruin his livelihood and destroy everything he has worked tirelessly for. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain and David Oyelowo(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark arranges a singing flash-mob with a group of dog walkers to prove that shared experiences like singing can make people happy and feel more bonded(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a semi-detached in Dover, Kent, and a parcel of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts explores the potential of a terrace in Tonypandy, south Wales(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dental director Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues who has two troublesome wisdom teeth that need to be removed in surgery(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. In this edition, the comedian is on a quest to discover the truth about technology finding it to be a story of phonetapping, detector vans and '60s microwaves, full of lively characters(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Anita Manning and experts Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw get into the panto spirit at Newark Antiques Fair for a festive edition of the show, helping the teams spend £300 on three items to take to auction in Derbyshire. Away from the contest, Anita joins a band for a seasonal sing-a-long and looks into the history of Christmas cards(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi is arrested when a stash of drugs are found in his car. Meanwhile, Daniel has to step in when he encounters a man who is gambling with his own life(n)

programme.title: The Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Part two of two. With police detectives seemingly no closer to closing the murder cases that have shaken Ballarat, their suspicion is aroused by some articles about unsolved cases in Ballarat - could these murders be linked to something long ago? Meanwhile, Jean's compassion is the only thing standing in the way of Matthew convicting the man who fell through the ceiling of the abandoned house. Australian mystery drama, starring Nadine Garner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman finds herself in the east Devon countryside with a couple who have £750,000 to find a more pastoral life and business. While in the county, Nicki goes deep into the woods to meet an artist who transforms fallen timber into beautiful bespoke winged sculptures(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry compete to design a family garden for a Cardiff couple, coming up with ideas that blend spaces for entertaining with room for two growing children(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts bring a 1930s portrait of Japanese martial arts master Yukio Tani back to life, rebuild a Victorian nursery chair and reconstruct an ornate porcelain clock(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Ahead of the final, Lord Sugar looks back on the tasks from this year's series and assesses the candidates' performances. The business tycoon also looks back on the boardroom bust-ups and goes through the best and worst decisions made by the contestants(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: Tom Allen meets this year's winner and runner-up, as well as Lord Sugar, to look back over the past 12 weeks of the business-based reality show. Featuring contributions from former contestants and special guests(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Ashleigh gets approved to buy a brand new house, and Sammyjo takes a big step in getting over Tom(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: In the final episode, Bev goes on the warpath as standards slip at the salon, and the Longlox girls look forward to Christmas and the future(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: This week, British pop royalty Ellie Goulding hands Michael her phone in Send To All, there is a sensational performance from the world's most beloved tenor, Andrea Bocelli, and two brothers from Coventry visit a relaxing spa but get the shock of their lives when they find themselves on stage at the London Palladium as they become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser goes into communities around Wales and creates choirs for people who feel isolated, beginning by setting one up in a rural area. Mark meets Dossie, a villager who now lives alone after her husband passed away, and Ella, who has recently moved back with her parents after university and is struggling to make new friends. Can he pull a group together not only to put on a performance of their lifetimes, but to help them make lasting connections with a real impact?(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a detached house in Gillingham, Kent, she first saw in 2017. Plus, a semi-detached in Walsall, West Midlands, and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A specialist surgeon travels to Shetland to carry out a complex operation, while a Fair Isle man hopes to get the all-clear after surgery to find out if cancer has been kept at bay(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, as consumers unbox, test and score a portion control plate, a music box and an innovative dog lead. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross visits York Theatre Royal to learn about the history of pantomime(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma struggles to make sense of what has happened, while Ruhma has surprise news for a pregnant woman in police custody(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Following reports of Flambeau's death, the clerical sleuth races to protect a crown that contains a nail from the crucifixion of Christ. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to South Wales with a couple who are looking for a country home after three decades of military housing, and have a budget of £430,000 to spend. Away from the property search, Jules dons his own army fatigues to discover how the beautiful Welsh wilderness prepares soldiers for battle(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry face a challenge in Liphook, Hampshire, where a couple have a big garden and a daughter with her own ideas on what makes the perfect outdoor space(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Lucia Scalisi tries to mend a serious tear in a portrait, William Kirk and Steven Fletcher join forces to fix a walking stick and Brenton West tackles a seaside telescope(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past five years, a radical new church has been promising hundreds of young people an escape from gangs and crime and a pathway to salvation through prosperity and success. SPAC Nation, the Salvation Proclaimers Anointed Church, holds services in hotels, conference halls and football grounds and leader Tobi Adegboyega has been courted by politicians and the media for his work in tackling gang violence. But Panorama has discovered how the church's outward show of concern for young people may hide a less than charitable appetite for money. Reporter Greg McKenzie investigates its fundraising activities and speaks to young worshippers who say they've been forced into debt whilst church leaders enjoy lavish lifestyles(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is sure Sharon and Jack are having an affair and sets out for revenge, despite Ben's efforts to convince him otherwise. Martin is on a high following another job with Tubbs, only for Sonia to bring him down when she quizzes him about his sudden wealth. Mick is frustrated when Ollie does not land a main role in the school nativity so he plans his own version - but Linda, Tina and Shirley make him realise it will not be quite so easy. Jay entrusts Stuart to manage the undertaker's(n)

programme.title: A Berry Royal Christmas
programme.desc: This one-off programme offers a unique look into charities that the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge support, as they invite Mary Berry to accompany them on four special visits. Mary also prepares a feast for a Christmas party hosted by the royal couple for the volunteers and gets help preparing the dishes from special guests including former Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain - and the Duke and Duchess themselves. Throughout the programme Mary also cooks some of her favourite Christmas recipes for viewers to try out at home(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The festive season arrives once again in the Brown household, and Father Damian has organised a Christmas decoration contest to support the local football team. Agnes is keen to enter, especially as it might mean getting one over her old nemesis, Hillary Nicholson, but is soon distracted by her new 'wi-fi assistant' and quite what to do with it(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The baby is due soon and Siobhan is upset with the nonstop arguing of both families, so Orla tries to come up with a solution. She suggests that everyone go away for a weekend break together to neighbouring Co Kerry and try to learn to get along for Siobhan and the baby's sake, but no-one seems convinced this is a good idea(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Pointless co-host Alexander Armstrong takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up comedian Jon Richardson one of two guests joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winners Judi Dench and Jennifer Hudson talk about the new film version of Cats, Hollywood stars Hugh Grant and Matthew McConaughey discuss their new action thriller The Gentlemen and everyone's favourite crooner Michael Buble also drops in for a chat. Plus, Coldplay offer an exclusive performance from their new album Everyday Life and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark explores the Welsh valleys in search of members for his choir, including a woman who was born in the Bahamas, and misses the strong sense of community(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A patient arrives in A&E with mysterious abdominal pains, and Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues, who has two wisdom teeth that need to be removed(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel supports Zara as she ends things with Adam, while Al and Jimmi have a threatening visitor at The Icon. Ayesha deals with the fall-out from a batch of dodgy pills on campus(n)

programme.title: The Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: First in a two-part sequel to The Doctor Blake Mysteries. The year is 1963, and Dr Lucien Blake is missing, presumed dead, leaving Jean behind and bereaved in a changing Ballarat. But when Chief Superintendent Matthew Lawson is confronted with two grisly murders, Jean is determined to prove that she is more than equal to her husband when it comes to solving such a mystery. After all, no one knows this town better than her. Crime drama, starring Nadine Garner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is house-hunting in Somerset with a couple who have a £675,000 budget for a rural home that must have annexe potential for their parents. During his time in the county, Jules visits the Royal Navy's Fleet Air Arm Museum, where he gets up close and personal with an ambitious project to rebuild a Second World War aircraft using reclaimed parts(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock helps to create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing an adult relaxation area for parents Michelle and Alistair(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A music box housed in a replica Swiss chalet, a miniature globe from the 1800s and a broken garnet bracelet are pieced back together by the experts. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil goes to extreme lengths to show he is not to be messed with, leaving Jack in terrible danger. Mick and Mitch plan their alternative nativity - and get help from an unexpected source. The Panesar brothers burst into the Slaters with some shocking news - they are buying their house - and Karen suggests Janet should enter a beauty contest, despite Honey's concerns(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lofty and Dom prepare to renew their wedding vows with some special guests - but Lofty has begun to question the validity of their marriage, and is forced to consider what he really wants out of life. Sacha's daughter gives some tough advice, and Chloe tries to decide what she wants to do about her pregnancy, and realises she must tell Ange everything if she is to find peace. Casualty stars Amanda Henderson and Jamie Davis make a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Julia must decide whether she is able to go through with her wedding day in light of what she has learned about Benjamin. She tries to confide in Della, only to discover that her daughter has come to empathise with her loyalty to Benjamin and been inspired to make a positive change in her own life. As she realises that cancelling the wedding risks causing her daughter pain, she turns to Ted, who tries to persuade her to give their relationship another chance. However, she rejects his fantasy of a happy past and forces him to come to terms with what he did to her(n)

programme.title: Inside the Cage: The Rise of Female Fighters
programme.desc: Annie Price meets the women fighting to the top in Mixed Martial Arts, one of the UK's fastest growing sports, finding out whether the risks of the sport are worth the reward. Annie meets Molly McCann, the first English woman to win in the Ultimate Fighting Championship, and Cory McKenna, a 19-year-old former student who has left her studies behind to become a full-time fighter(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is both payday and Halloween party time at the salon. Tom gets a tepid welcome from the girls as Sammyjo tries to find out where she stands(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Saffron tells Nan about her budding romance. There is another shock for Sammyjo, and she seeks outside help to figure out what to do(n)

programme.title: A Most Violent Year (2014)
programme.desc: New York City during the winter of 1981 is a dangerous place to be, but one immigrant is determined to expand his business and support his family. Unfortunately, the unrelenting rise in violent crime threatens to ruin his livelihood and destroy everything he has worked tirelessly for. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain and David Oyelowo(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past five years, a radical new church has been promising hundreds of young people an escape from gangs and crime and a pathway to salvation through prosperity and success. SPAC Nation, the Salvation Proclaimers Anointed Church, holds services in hotels, conference halls and football grounds and leader Tobi Adegboyega has been courted by politicians and the media for his work in tackling gang violence. But Panorama has discovered how the church's outward show of concern for young people may hide a less than charitable appetite for money. Reporter Greg McKenzie investigates its fundraising activities and speaks to young worshippers who say they've been forced into debt whilst church leaders enjoy lavish lifestyles(n)

programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

The stars of The Real Housewives of Cheshire take a blind taste test to see if they can tell the difference between supermarket sparkling wine and real champagne. programme.title: Sticks and Stones
programme.desc: Thomas feels under pressure to succeed in all aspects of his life, so arranges three vitally important encounters on the same night. First he attempts to ingratiate himself with his boss over a drink, then tries to persuade an old friend to become a new client, and finally has dinner with his father-in-law. However, his attempt to do too much has terrible consequences, causing his life to spiral even further out of control(n)

programme.title: The Dirty War on the NHS
programme.desc: Documentary examining plans to sell off the National Health Service. The film explores the history of the world's first people's health service and how it has provided a vital safety net to generation of people, and examines efforts to introduce an insurance-based healthcare system, which in the US, has denied vital medical treatment to millions of people. Filmed in both countries, the film warns of danger to the fundamental human right to medical care(n)

programme.title: Bancroft

programme.title: The Dirty War on the NHS
programme.desc: Documentary examining plans to sell off the National Health Service. The film explores the history of the world's first people's health service and how it has provided a vital safety net to generation of people, and examines efforts to introduce an insurance-based healthcare system, which in the US, has denied vital medical treatment to millions of people. Filmed in both countries, the film warns of danger to the fundamental human right to medical care(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy tries to impress Miller by going it alone, while the arrival of tax inspector Derek Ford gets the residents of Aidensfield panicked. Vernon bears the brunt of Ford's wrath when his dodgy dealings and poor record-keeping are exposed, convincing the wheeler dealer the only way to avoid trouble is to fake his own suicide. Rural drama, guest starring James Weaver, with Mark Jordon, Geoffrey Hughes and John Duttine(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.title: The Bletchley Circle: San Francisco
programme.desc: A bloodied and battered stranger collapses on Millie and Jean's doorstep, dragging them into a mysterious warring underworld. In a race against time, the four women must unravel the patterns behind a series of attacks in order to avoid becoming the next victims. Drama set during the mid-1950s, following four women gifted with extraordinary intelligence, capacity for pattern recognition and decryption. Crystal Balint, Rachael Stirling, Julie Graham and Chanelle Peloso star(n)

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: University lecturer Dr Leah Dale has always prided herself on her academic integrity, so when final-year student Rose Vaughan submits a suspiciously top-grade essay, Leah is quick to call her out. But there's more going on than meets the eye as Rose takes the challenge as a personal affront, and what begins as a seemingly open-and-shut case of academic deception soon spirals out of control. Drama, starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb(n)

programme.desc: Derek starts at Hawthorne's, The Gazette runs a story on Jack, dubbing him The Bird Man of Weatherfield, and Deirdre is pleased to see Ken again(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita agrees to take Nick on as a paperboy, Deirdre is surprised to find Denise stand-offish, and Raquel suggests to Des that they entertain Tanya and Alex(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Clare finds herself under suspicion when a wild party ends in tragedy with a drugs-related death, and David is put in a tricky situation when his Aunty Peggy arrives in Aidensfield and sets up an illegal vodka still(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason(n)

programme.title: The Bletchley Circle: San Francisco
programme.desc: Having uncovered a covert system of corruption inside the police department, the women must go it alone, crafting a complex sting operation in order to save Edward and draw out two warring factions, thus ensuring justice is done. Millie and Jean's lives are upended when they are questioned in connection with Edward's 'communist' activities, so time becomes precious, as the duo seem set to be deported back to the United Kingdom. Drama set during the mid-1950s, starring Crystal Balint, Rachael Stirling, Julie Graham and Chanelle Peloso(n)

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: With relations becoming increasingly hostile, Leah resorts to more extreme measures to expose Rose, and as tensions flare between Leah and Adam, could it be that her husband is taking Rose's side? Later, Rose seems to pursue a more personal retaliation against Leah, but who is telling the truth? Katherine Kelly, Molly Windsor and Tom Goodman-Hill star(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George celebrates his 24th wedding anniversary by presenting Mildred with a carriage clock. However, he has purchased it cut-price from a dubious source, and when the Fourmiles return from holiday they find their home burgled, and among the missing items is a carriage clock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry.
